title: taraxacum spetanum
intro: Taraxacum spetanum, also known as the Spetan dandelion, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Balkan Peninsula. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure, improving liver function, and supporting digestive health. Taraxacum spetanum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of conditions, including urinary tract infections and skin disorders.
